description: Sets up the Runneth permission system. Default is PERMISSIVE mode (everyone resolved writes anywhere except others home base, with author: @<handle> attribution). STRICT mode is an opt-in upgrade with member-confined writes, locked paths, and per-space writer maps — reached through an educational walkthrough and a 6-question interview that adapts to org shape (solo, small team, single brand, multi-brand agency, dept structure, or custom).

requires.runtime: any Runneth sandbox; jq must be installed

post-install.name: Run the five-phase deployment
post-install.description: Invokes the skill's full deployment: pre-flight scan, summary + confirmation, file writes, post-deploy verification, post-deploy setup checklist.

post-install.name: Introduce the new capability
post-install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.

changelog.notes: Major rewrite. Strict permissions is now opt-in instead of default. New PERMISSIVE default mode: anyone resolved through Slack or Motion web writes anywhere under /agent/ except another persons home base, every durable write gets `author: @<handle>` attribution. Removes member confinement, locked paths, and blocked-action flow from the default install. STRICT mode reached through Phase 2 educational walkthrough + Phase 3 conversational interview (6 questions: mode, org shape, first admin, admin Slack channel, per-space writer maps, locked-path extras). Phase 4 reads the proposal back to the admin for confirmation before any writes. Phase 5 generates a mode-specific permissions.md from a permissive or strict template plus interview-driven sections (per-space writer map, extra locked paths). New mode.json records the chosen mode, org shape, and interview answers for idempotent reconfigure. Org-shape scaffolding adapts the brain folder layout: solo gets notes/decisions, small_team gets shared/, single_brand gets brand/, multi_brand_agency gets brands/<brand>/ per brand, dept_structure gets teams/<team>/ per team, custom takes admin-supplied folder names. v2.x migration paths preserved (keep strict + add mode.json bookkeeping, or switch to permissive). v3.0 protocol pointer in user.md is mode-aware. Refs: PDEC-7817.

Two changes: (1) renamed workspace-map.json to organization-map.json (clearer: it is the organization-wide identity registry, not a workspace-scoped artifact); env override variable renamed RUNNETH_WORKSPACE_MAP to RUNNETH_ORG_MAP. (2) motion-whoami.sh is now Neon-only: queries the Neon agent_conversation table via a new motion-whoami-neon.py helper (invoked through secret run --env DATABASE_URL=NEON_DATABASE_URL) for authoritative, zero-lag identity resolution. No SQLite fallback — on Neon failure the script exits non-zero and the permissions layer treats the user as unknown (no writes) per the §2.Unknown rule. Accepts CONVERSATION_ID from env in addition to cwd-basename. Fixes the failure mode where brand-new conversations could not be resolved because the live conversations.db is a 0-byte placeholder until the backup cycle. Migration of existing orgs is deferred. Refs: PDEC-7817.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.
Initial install-config. Replaces ad-hoc skill-invoke install with the standard protocol.
